items.headline: US Equities Recoup Early Losses Amid Geopolitical Volatility, Inflation Cools to 2.1%
items.summary: US stock markets experienced significant intraday volatility on Thursday, April 2, 2026, initially declining sharply due to heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and surging oil prices. However, major indices pared most of their early losses to close slightly down or near flat. Positive economic data, including a cooling of inflation to 2.1% in March and an upward revision of 2026 GDP growth to 2.4%, provided some underlying support. The Federal Reserve, while acknowledging economic stabilization, indicated interest rate cuts are unlikely until late in the year. Geopolitical developments and commodity prices remained key drivers of market sentiment throughout the day. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Sky Quarry ($SKYQ)
items.summary: Topped gainers amid ongoing volatility from recent reverse split and Nasdaq notices.

items.headline: PMGC HOLDINGS INC ($ELAB)
items.summary: Experienced a significant decrease in stock price during Thursday's session.

items.headline: INOVIO PHARMACEUTICALS INC ($INO)
items.summary: Declined following a press release regarding a securities class action deadline.

items.headline: US Inflation Cools to 2.1% in March, Aligns with Fed Targets
items.summary: Fresh macroeconomic data for April 2, 2026, shows headline inflation eased to 2.1% in March, closely matching the Federal Reserve's long-term objectives. GDP growth projections for the 2026 fiscal year were revised upward to 2.4%. The Fed signaled that significant interest rate cuts are improbable until late in the year, maintaining a cautious stance amidst a 'geopolitical pause.'

items.headline: Oil Prices Remain Elevated as Geopolitical Tensions Persist
items.summary: Crude oil prices remained elevated on Thursday after President Donald Trump vowed to continue military action against Iran, diminishing hopes for a swift de-escalation of the Middle East conflict. Benchmark U.S. crude surged to $108.82 per barrel, while Brent crude jumped to $105.99. The ongoing tensions and curtailment of shipping through the Strait of Hormuz continue to drive market volatility.

items.headline: Energy Sector Leads, Tech and Discretionary Lag Amidst Market Rotation
items.summary: On April 2, 2026, the Energy sector ($XLE) led gains, advancing 2.66%. Conversely, Technology ($XLK) declined 2.28%, Communication Services ($XLC) fell 1.56%, and Industrials ($XLI) dropped 1.77%. Consumer discretionary stocks also underperformed. This trend reflects a broader market rotation out of the 'Magnificent Seven' mega-cap tech stocks and into value, small-caps, and dividend payers, a shift observed throughout Q1 2026.

items.headline: Focus Shifts to Q1 Earnings Season with 12% S&P 500 Growth Expected
items.summary: Attention is turning to the upcoming first-quarter earnings season, which is anticipated to be a critical period for markets. Analysts are forecasting a 12% S&P 500 earnings growth for 2026. Key reports from major companies like JPMorgan, Bank of America, TSMC, Netflix, and UnitedHealth are expected in April.
